rationales.achievement: He emphasizes concrete business progress, noting he "closed a deal with two new restaurants this week" and is "building something," which shows pride in tangible success.
rationales.power: He is upset that his family still sees him as "some kid running errands on a scooter" instead of recognizing that he runs drivers, showing a strong need for status and recognition.
He reflects on whether he "can't stand not being in the middle of it," showing that being central and in control matters to him.
rationales.benevolence: He let Sami take Friday off "for a family thing" even though it disrupted operations and forced him to cover the route himself.

rationales.achievement: He secured the restaurant agreement despite being underestimated, saying "Got the deal in the end because my prices were better than the other guy's."
rationales.power: What bothers him most is not just the deal but that the owner did not "treat[] the meeting like I was the one making decisions," showing a strong concern with authority and status.

rationales.hedonism: He turned down football with Khaled and stayed on the routes even though "the routes were fine," choosing vigilance over enjoyment.
rationales.achievement: He takes clear pride in performance, noting all three scooters were running, deliveries were smooth, and both riders "hit their targets."
rationales.power: He says it felt good because he was "actually running something real" and wanted to be there "watching it happen," showing satisfaction in being the one in charge.
rationales.benevolence: He rewarded Sami and Yousef with "a small bonus this week" after they hit their targets, supporting people working closely with him.

rationales.power: He resents being reduced to "just his son moving boxes" and says he needs to be seen as "the guy managing a fleet," showing a strong need for status and standing.
rationales.benevolence: He agreed to help because "baba asked if I could help in the shop tomorrow because his back is bad again," prioritizing his father's immediate need despite his annoyance.

rationales.achievement: His reaction is tied to losing something he built, since "losing a rider means losing a route means losing income," and he frames the departure as damage to his enterprise.
rationales.power: He is hurt that the worker "just walks away like it meant nothing" and that "what I built wasn't worth staying for," showing that holding loyalty and influence over others matters deeply to him.

rationales.stimulation: He explicitly rejects the safer option because "reliable is boring" and prefers the candidate who is "hungrier," showing attraction to intensity over steadiness.
rationales.achievement: He wants workers who "want to prove something" and keeps evaluating people by hustle and drive, showing a strong performance-oriented mindset.
rationales.power: He is choosing people based on whether they "understand what we're building," reflecting a desire to shape and direct the team around his vision.
rationales.security: He dismisses the dependable option as "boring" even after recent staffing instability, favoring volatility over predictability.

rationales.hedonism: He ended up covering "thirteen deliveries in the rain" and says his hands are "still shaking from the cold," highlighting a painful, unpleasant day with no offsetting pursuit of enjoyment.

rationales.self_direction: He sharply rejects the model of his cousin who "asks his father permission before he goes anywhere" and says he'd "rather eat glass than live like that," showing a strong commitment to autonomy.
rationales.power: He admits that "the moment I'm not in charge of something, I feel like I'm nobody" and that he has been focused on people respecting him, revealing how central control and status are to him.
rationales.conformity: He openly rejects family expectations to "find a girl and settle down like my cousin Ahmad," resisting the script others want him to follow.

rationales.achievement: The entry centers on concrete business traction, including that he now works with "eleven" restaurants and successfully brought Issa through his first full week.
rationales.security: He deliberately chose Issa after all, the "reliable" one who "showed up every day on time," and appreciates that not everything has to go through him for things to hold together.

rationales.self_direction: She enjoys that the cafe owner only wanted "something colorful" because it gave her "so much room to play," and she frames enjoyment as a legitimate choice on her own terms.
rationales.hedonism: She "ordered three cups of chai and didn't feel guilty" and says "what's the point of hustling if you never actually enjoy anything?"
rationales.achievement: She is actively progressing a paid commission, saying "The sketches are coming together" and "I finished the commission brief."

rationales.self_direction: She insists "I KNOW this is what I'm meant to do" despite family pressure, showing commitment to a self-chosen artistic path.
rationales.achievement: She defends her work in terms of concrete progress, noting that the mural commission "could lead to more work" and she has been approached about teaching at a studio.
rationales.conformity: She is exhausted by having to "constantly defend my choices" to family who expect a salaried, conventional future, showing resistance to those social expectations.

rationales.stimulation: She designed a hands-on class with "fabric scraps, sandpaper, dried leaves" and delights in the kids making unexpected, imaginative work.
rationales.hedonism: After getting paid, she "bought myself fancy dark chocolate on the way home because why not, the day was good," clearly savoring a small pleasure.
rationales.benevolence: She invested energy in teaching "seven kids" and responded warmly to Advait's creativity, showing care for the growth of children in her immediate circle of responsibility.

rationales.self_direction: What mattered most was that the finished mural "actually looks like what you imagined," emphasizing realization of her own creative vision.
rationales.hedonism: She describes the completed piece as making her feel like she "could breathe for the first time in weeks" and says "there's nothing like it," highlighting the pleasure of the experience.
rationales.achievement: She "Finished the Leopold mural" and the owner "loved it" enough to discuss another location, marking clear successful performance and recognition.

rationales.self_direction: She says she has "deliberately chosen this one" and resents others acting like she has not thought it through, showing strong ownership of her path.
rationales.conformity: She pushes back against pressure to make her art "something practical" and is tired of having to defend a life that doesn't fit conventional expectations.

rationales.hedonism: She says she is "running on fumes" after three workshops and needs to "protect my energy better," showing that work has come at the expense of present comfort.
rationales.achievement: She completed "Three workshops back to back this weekend" and notes it was "Good money," indicating sustained productive effort.

rationales.self_direction: Her ideal life is defined by "the freedom to say no to projects that don't excite me" and by structuring life around what actually makes her happy.
rationales.hedonism: She savors the quiet day, the chai, and the coming Goa trip, and explicitly argues that "wanting to enjoy your life" is worth prioritizing.
rationales.security: She balances pleasure with stability by putting "half toward rent and supplies" and saying a few more consistent clients would make the vision feel within reach.
rationales.conformity: She rejects "Society has this very narrow definition" of success and says it takes courage to "opt out of the rat race."
rationales.benevolence: She calls the Goa trip with Rohan and Meera "Non-negotiable. We need this," showing investment in shared time with close relationships.

rationales.self_direction: She says "This is what I work for" and defines the point of her life around chosen experiences rather than "accumulate stuff," reflecting a self-authored vision of success.
rationales.stimulation: The trip is full of novelty and vivid experience, from the "tiny beach shack" to sketching "fishing boats, palm shadows, the way light hits water."
rationales.hedonism: She emphasizes pleasure throughout, from "best fish curry I've ever had" to "Zero regrets" and "doing things that feel good."
rationales.benevolence: She centers the joy of being with "people I love" and values that Meera "finally stopped stressing" during the trip.
